The provided element IDs must exist within the ad-hoc sub-process instance identified by the provided adHocSubProcessInstanceKey. public Task ActivateAdHocSubProcessActivitiesAsync(ElementInstanceKey adHocSubProcessInstanceKey, AdHocSubProcessActivateActivitiesInstruction body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters adHocSubProcessInstanceKey ElementInstanceKey body AdHocSubProcessActivateActivitiesInstruction ct CancellationToken Returns Task Examples public static async Task ActivateAdHocSubProcessActivitiesExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); await client.ActivateAdHocSubProcessActivitiesAsync( ElementInstanceKey.AssumeExists("123456"), new AdHocSubProcessActivateActivitiesInstruction()); } Remarks Operation: activateAdHocSubProcessActivities

As a cancellation includes more than just the removal of the process instance resource, the cancellation resource must be posted. Cancellation can wait on listener-related processing; when that processing does not complete in time, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats. public Task CancelProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey processInstanceKey, CancelProcessInstanceRequest body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters processInstanceKey ProcessInstanceKey body CancelProcessInstanceRequest ct CancellationToken Returns Task Examples public static async Task CancelProcessInstanceExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); await client.CancelProcessInstanceAsync( ProcessInstanceKey.AssumeExists("123456"), new CancelProcessInstanceRequest()); } Remarks Operation: cancelProcessInstance

Since only ACTIVE root instances can be cancelled, any given filters for state and parentProcessInstanceKey are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.title: Method CreateDocumentsAsync | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Method CreateDocumentsAsync Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll CreateDocumentsAsync(MultipartFormDataContent, string?, CancellationToken) Upload multiple documents Upload multiple documents to the Camunda 8 cluster. The caller must provide a file name for each document, which will be used in case of a multi-status response to identify which documents failed to upload. The file name can be provided in the Content-Disposition header of the file part or in the fileName field of the metadata. You can add a parallel array of metadata objects. These are matched with the files based on index, and must have the same length as the files array. To pass homogenous metadata for all files, spread the metadata over the metadata array. A filename value provided explicitly via the metadata array in the request overrides the Content-Disposition header of the file part. In case of a multi-status response, the response body will contain a list of DocumentBatchProblemDetail objects, each of which contains the file name of the document that failed to upload and the reason for the failure. The client can choose to retry the whole batch or individual documents based on the response. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, Azure, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production) public Task<DocumentCreationBatchResponse> CreateDocumentsAsync(MultipartFormDataContent content, string? storeId = null, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters content MultipartFormDataContent storeId string ct CancellationToken Returns Task<DocumentCreationBatchResponse> Remarks Operation: createDocuments

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Method CreateJobWorker Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll CreateJobWorker(JobWorkerConfig, JobHandler) Create a job worker that polls for and processes jobs of the specified type. The handler receives an ActivatedJob and returns variables to auto-complete. Throw BpmnErrorException for BPMN errors, JobFailureException for explicit failures, or any other exception to auto-fail with retries - 1. public JobWorker CreateJobWorker(JobWorkerConfig config, JobHandler handler) Parameters config JobWorkerConfig Worker configuration (job type, timeout, concurrency). handler JobHandler Async handler that processes each job. Return output variables (or null) to complete. Returns JobWorker The running JobWorker instance. CreateJobWorker(JobWorkerConfig, Func<ActivatedJob, CancellationToken, Task>) Create a job worker with a handler that doesn't return output variables. The job is auto-completed with no variables on success. public JobWorker CreateJobWorker(JobWorkerConfig config, Func<ActivatedJob, CancellationToken, Task> handler) Parameters config JobWorkerConfig handler Func<ActivatedJob, CancellationToken, Task> Returns JobWorker

This will delete the historic data from secondary storage. Only process instances in a final state (COMPLETED or TERMINATED) can be deleted. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). public Task<BatchOperationCreatedResult> DeleteProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceDeletionBatchOperationRequest body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters body ProcessInstanceDeletionBatchOperationRequest ct CancellationToken Returns Task<BatchOperationCreatedResult> Examples public static async Task DeleteProcessInstancesBatchOperationExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); var result = await client.DeleteProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync( new ProcessInstanceDeletionBatchOperationRequest()); Console.WriteLine($"Batch operation key: {result.BatchOperationKey}"); } Remarks Operation: deleteProcessInstancesBatchOperation

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.title: Method DeleteResourceAsync | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Method DeleteResourceAsync Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll DeleteResourceAsync(ResourceKey, DeleteResourceRequest, CancellationToken) Delete resource Deletes a deployed resource. This can be a process definition, decision requirements definition, or form definition deployed using the deploy resources endpoint. Specify the resource you want to delete in the resourceKey parameter. Once a resource has been deleted it cannot be recovered. If the resource needs to be available again, a new deployment of the resource is required. By default, only the resource itself is deleted from the runtime state. To also delete the historic data associated with a resource, set the deleteHistory flag in the request body to true. The historic data is deleted asynchronously via a batch operation. The details of the created batch operation are included in the response. Note that history deletion is only supported for process resources; for other resource types this flag is ignored and no history will be deleted. public Task<DeleteResourceResponse> DeleteResourceAsync(ResourceKey resourceKey, DeleteResourceRequest body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters resourceKey ResourceKey body DeleteResourceRequest ct CancellationToken Returns Task<DeleteResourceResponse> Examples public static async Task DeleteResourceExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); await client.DeleteResourceAsync( ResourceKey.AssumeExists("123456"), new DeleteResourceRequest()); } Remarks Operation: deleteResource

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.title: Method MigrateProcessInstanceAsync | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Method MigrateProcessInstanceAsync Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll MigrateProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ProcessInstanceMigrationInstruction, CancellationToken) Migrate process instance Migrates a process instance to a new process definition. This request can contain multiple mapping instructions to define mapping between the active process instance's elements and target process definition elements. Use this to upgrade a process instance to a new version of a process or to a different process definition, e.g. to keep your running instances up-to-date with the latest process improvements. public Task MigrateProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey processInstanceKey, ProcessInstanceMigrationInstruction body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters processInstanceKey ProcessInstanceKey body ProcessInstanceMigrationInstruction ct CancellationToken Returns Task Examples public static async Task MigrateProcessInstanceExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); await client.MigrateProcessInstanceAsync( ProcessInstanceKey.AssumeExists("123456"), new ProcessInstanceMigrationInstruction { TargetProcessDefinitionKey = ProcessDefinitionKey.AssumeExists("789012"), }); } Remarks Operation: migrateProcessInstance

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.title: Method MigrateProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Method MigrateProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll MigrateProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceMigrationBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken) Migrate process instances (batch) Migrate multiple process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can be migrated, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). public Task<BatchOperationCreatedResult> MigrateProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceMigrationBatchOperationRequest body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters body ProcessInstanceMigrationBatchOperationRequest ct CancellationToken Returns Task<BatchOperationCreatedResult> Examples public static async Task MigrateProcessInstancesBatchOperationExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); var result = await client.MigrateProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync( new ProcessInstanceMigrationBatchOperationRequest { Filter = new ProcessInstanceFilter(), MigrationPlan = new ProcessInstanceMigrationBatchOperationPlan { TargetProcessDefinitionKey = ProcessDefinitionKey.AssumeExists("456789"), }, }); Console.WriteLine($"Batch operation key: {result.BatchOperationKey}"); } Remarks Operation: migrateProcessInstancesBatchOperation

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.title: Method ModifyProcessInstanceAsync | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Method ModifyProcessInstanceAsync Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll ModifyProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ProcessInstanceModificationInstruction, CancellationToken) Modify process instance Modifies a running process instance. This request can contain multiple instructions to activate an element of the process or to terminate an active instance of an element. Use this to repair a process instance that is stuck on an element or took an unintended path. For example, because an external system is not available or doesn't respond as expected. public Task ModifyProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey processInstanceKey, ProcessInstanceModificationInstruction body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters processInstanceKey ProcessInstanceKey body ProcessInstanceModificationInstruction ct CancellationToken Returns Task Examples public static async Task ModifyProcessInstanceExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); await client.ModifyProcessInstanceAsync( ProcessInstanceKey.AssumeExists("123456"), new ProcessInstanceModificationInstruction()); } Remarks Operation: modifyProcessInstance

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.title: Method ModifyProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Method ModifyProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll ModifyProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceModificationBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken) Modify process instances (batch) Modify multiple process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can be modified, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. In contrast to single modification operation, it is not possible to add variable instructions or modify by element key. It is only possible to use the element id of the source and target. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). public Task<BatchOperationCreatedResult> ModifyProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceModificationBatchOperationRequest body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters body ProcessInstanceModificationBatchOperationRequest ct CancellationToken Returns Task<BatchOperationCreatedResult> Examples public static async Task ModifyProcessInstancesBatchOperationExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); var result = await client.ModifyProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync( new ProcessInstanceModificationBatchOperationRequest()); Console.WriteLine($"Batch operation key: {result.BatchOperationKey}"); } Remarks Operation: modifyProcessInstancesBatchOperation

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Method PinClockAsync Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll PinClockAsync(ClockPinRequest, CancellationToken) Pin internal clock (alpha) Set a precise, static time for the Zeebe engine's internal clock. When the clock is pinned, it remains at the specified time and does not advance. To change the time, the clock must be pinned again with a new timestamp. This endpoint is an alpha feature and may be subject to change in future releases. public Task PinClockAsync(ClockPinRequest body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters body ClockPinRequest ct CancellationToken Returns Task Examples public static async Task PinClockExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); await client.PinClockAsync(new ClockPinRequest { Timestamp = 1700000000000, }); } Remarks Operation: pinClock

Messages are published to specific partitions computed from their correlation keys. Messages can be buffered. The endpoint does not wait for a correlation result. Use the message correlation endpoint for such use cases. public Task<MessagePublicationResult> PublishMessageAsync(MessagePublicationRequest body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters body MessagePublicationRequest ct CancellationToken Returns Task<MessagePublicationResult> Examples public static async Task PublishMessageExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); var result = await client.PublishMessageAsync(new MessagePublicationRequest { Name = "paymentReceived", CorrelationKey = "order-123", TimeToLive = 60000, }); Console.WriteLine($"Message key: {result.MessageKey}"); } Remarks Operation: publishMessage

The variable must exist, otherwise a 404 error is returned. public Task<ClusterVariableResult> UpdateTenantClusterVariableAsync(TenantId tenantId, string name, UpdateClusterVariableRequest body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters tenantId TenantId name string body UpdateClusterVariableRequest ct CancellationToken Returns Task<ClusterVariableResult> Examples public static async Task UpdateTenantClusterVariableExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); var result = await client.UpdateTenantClusterVariableAsync( TenantId.AssumeExists("acme-corp"), "my-variable", new UpdateClusterVariableRequest { Value = "updated-tenant-value", }); Console.WriteLine($"Updated variable: {result.Name}"); } Remarks Operation: updateTenantClusterVariable

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.title: Method UpdateUserAsync | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Method UpdateUserAsync Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll UpdateUserAsync(Username, UserUpdateRequest, CancellationToken) Update user Updates a user. public Task<UpdateUserResponse> UpdateUserAsync(Username username, UserUpdateRequest body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters username Username body UserUpdateRequest ct CancellationToken Returns Task<UpdateUserResponse> Examples public static async Task UpdateUserExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); await client.UpdateUserAsync( Username.AssumeExists("jdoe"), new UserUpdateRequest { Name = "Jane Smith", Email = "jsmith@example.com", }); } Remarks Operation: updateUser

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.title: Method UpdateUserTaskAsync | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Method UpdateUserTaskAsync Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll UpdateUserTaskAsync(UserTaskKey, UserTaskUpdateRequest, CancellationToken) Update user task Update a user task with the given key. Updates wait for blocking task listeners on this lifecycle transition. If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats. public Task UpdateUserTaskAsync(UserTaskKey userTaskKey, UserTaskUpdateRequest body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters userTaskKey UserTaskKey body UserTaskUpdateRequest ct CancellationToken Returns Task Examples public static async Task UpdateUserTaskExample() { using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); await client.UpdateUserTaskAsync( UserTaskKey.AssumeExists("123456"), new UserTaskUpdateRequest()); } Remarks Operation: updateUserTask

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.title: Class CamundaClient | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.CamundaCli.summary: Class CamundaClient Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll Primary Camunda client. Provides typed methods for all Camunda 8 REST API operations. Auto-generated operation methods are added in the Generated/ partial class files. This class provides the infrastructure: configuration, auth, retry, backpressure. public class CamundaClient : IDisposable, IAsyncDisposable Inheritance object CamundaClient Implements IDisposable IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods TypedVariables.DeserializeAs<T>(object?, JsonSerializerOptions?) Constructors CamundaClient(CamundaOptions?) Create a new CamundaClient with the given options. Properties Config The current hydrated configuration (read-only). Methods ActivateAdHocSubProcessActivitiesAsync(ElementInstanceKey, AdHocSubProcessActivateActivitiesInstruction, CancellationToken) Activate activities within an ad-hoc sub-process Activates selected activities within an ad-hoc sub-process identified by element ID. The provided element IDs must exist within the ad-hoc sub-process instance identified by the provided adHocSubProcessInstanceKey. ActivateJobsAsync(JobActivationRequest, CancellationToken) Activate jobs Iterate through all known partitions and activate jobs up to the requested maximum. AssignClientToGroupAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Assign a client to a group Assigns a client to a group, making it a member of the group. Members of the group inherit the group authorizations, roles, and tenant assignments. AssignClientToTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken) Assign a client to a tenant Assign the client to the specified tenant. The client can then access tenant data and perform authorized actions. AssignGroupToTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken) Assign a group to a tenant Assigns a group to a specified tenant. Group members (users, clients) can then access tenant data and perform authorized actions. AssignMappingRuleToGroupAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Assign a mapping rule to a group Assigns a mapping rule to a group. AssignMappingRuleToTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken) Assign a mapping rule to a tenant Assign a single mapping rule to a specified tenant. AssignRoleToClientAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Assign a role to a client Assigns the specified role to the client. The client will inherit the authorizations associated with this role. AssignRoleToGroupAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Assign a role to a group Assigns the specified role to the group. Every member of the group (user or client) will inherit the authorizations associated with this role. AssignRoleToMappingRuleAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Assign a role to a mapping rule Assigns a role to a mapping rule. AssignRoleToTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken) Assign a role to a tenant Assigns a role to a specified tenant. Users, Clients or Groups, that have the role assigned, will get access to the tenant's data and can perform actions according to their authorizations. AssignRoleToUserAsync(string, Username, CancellationToken) Assign a role to a user Assigns the specified role to the user. The user will inherit the authorizations associated with this role. AssignUserTaskAsync(UserTaskKey, UserTaskAssignmentRequest, CancellationToken) Assign user task Assigns a user task with the given key to the given assignee. Assignment waits for blocking task listeners on this lifecycle transition. If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats. AssignUserToGroupAsync(string, Username, CancellationToken) Assign a user to a group Assigns a user to a group, making the user a member of the group. Group members inherit the group authorizations, roles, and tenant assignments. AssignUserToTenantAsync(TenantId, Username, CancellationToken) Assign a user to a tenant Assign a single user to a specified tenant. The user can then access tenant data and perform authorized actions. BroadcastSignalAsync(SignalBroadcastRequest, CancellationToken) Broadcast signal Broadcasts a signal. CancelBatchOperationAsync(BatchOperationKey, CancellationToken) Cancel Batch operation Cancels a running batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). CancelProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, CancelProcessInstanceRequest, CancellationToken) Cancel process instance Cancels a running process instance. As a cancellation includes more than just the removal of the process instance resource, the cancellation resource must be posted. Cancellation can wait on listener-related processing; when that processing does not complete in time, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats. CancelProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceCancellationBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken) Cancel process instances (batch) Cancels multiple running process instances. Since only ACTIVE root instances can be cancelled, any given filters for state and parentProcessInstanceKey are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). CompleteJobAsync(JobKey, JobCompletionRequest, CancellationToken) Complete job Complete a job with the given payload, which allows completing the associated service task. CompleteUserTaskAsync(UserTaskKey, UserTaskCompletionRequest, CancellationToken) Complete user task Completes a user task with the given key. Completion waits for blocking task listeners on this lifecycle transition. If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats. CorrelateMessageAsync(MessageCorrelationRequest, CancellationToken) Correlate message Publishes a message and correlates it to a subscription. If correlation is successful it will return the first process instance key the message correlated with. The message is not buffered. Use the publish message endpoint to send messages that can be buffered. Create(CamundaOptions?) Create a new CamundaClient. CreateAdminUserAsync(UserRequest, CancellationToken) Create admin user Creates a new user and assigns the admin role to it. This endpoint is only usable when users are managed in the Orchestration Cluster and while no user is assigned to the admin role. CreateAuthorizationAsync(AuthorizationRequest, CancellationToken) Create authorization Create the authorization. CreateDeploymentAsync(MultipartFormDataContent, CancellationToken) Deploy resources Deploys one or more resources (e.g. processes, decision models, or forms). This is an atomic call, i.e. either all resources are deployed or none of them are. CreateDocumentAsync(MultipartFormDataContent, string?, DocumentId?, CancellationToken) Upload document Upload a document to the Camunda 8 cluster. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, Azure, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production) CreateDocumentLinkAsync(DocumentId, DocumentLinkRequest, string?, string?, CancellationToken) Create document link Create a link to a document in the Camunda 8 cluster. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, Azure, GCP CreateDocumentsAsync(MultipartFormDataContent, string?, CancellationToken) Upload multiple documents Upload multiple documents to the Camunda 8 cluster. The caller must provide a file name for each document, which will be used in case of a multi-status response to identify which documents failed to upload. The file name can be provided in the Content-Disposition header of the file part or in the fileName field of the metadata. You can add a parallel array of metadata objects. These are matched with the files based on index, and must have the same length as the files array. To pass homogenous metadata for all files, spread the metadata over the metadata array. A filename value provided explicitly via the metadata array in the request overrides the Content-Disposition header of the file part. In case of a multi-status response, the response body will contain a list of DocumentBatchProblemDetail objects, each of which contains the file name of the document that failed to upload and the reason for the failure. The client can choose to retry the whole batch or individual documents based on the response. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, Azure, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production) CreateElementInstanceVariablesAsync(ElementInstanceKey, SetVariableRequest, CancellationToken) Update element instance variables Updates all the variables of a particular scope (for example, process instance, element instance) with the given variable data. Specify the element instance in the elementInstanceKey parameter. Variable updates can be delayed by listener-related processing; if processing exceeds the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats. CreateGlobalClusterVariableAsync(CreateClusterVariableRequest, CancellationToken) Create a global-scoped cluster variable Create a global-scoped cluster variable. CreateGlobalTaskListenerAsync(CreateGlobalTaskListenerRequest, CancellationToken) Create global user task listener Create a new global user task listener. CreateGroupAsync(GroupCreateRequest, CancellationToken) Create group Create a new group. CreateJobWorker(JobWorkerConfig, JobHandler) Create a job worker that polls for and processes jobs of the specified type. The handler receives an ActivatedJob and returns variables to auto-complete. Throw BpmnErrorException for BPMN errors, JobFailureException for explicit failures, or any other exception to auto-fail with retries - 1. CreateJobWorker(JobWorkerConfig, Func<ActivatedJob, CancellationToken, Task>) Create a job worker with a handler that doesn't return output variables. The job is auto-completed with no variables on success. CreateMappingRuleAsync(MappingRuleCreateRequest, CancellationToken) Create mapping rule Create a new mapping rule CreateProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceCreationInstruction, CancellationToken) Create process instance Creates and starts an instance of the specified process. The process definition to use to create the instance can be specified either using its unique key (as returned by Deploy resources), or using the BPMN process id and a version. Waits for the completion of the process instance before returning a result when awaitCompletion is enabled. CreateRoleAsync(RoleCreateRequest, CancellationToken) Create role Create a new role. CreateTenantAsync(TenantCreateRequest, CancellationToken) Create tenant Creates a new tenant. CreateTenantClusterVariableAsync(TenantId, CreateClusterVariableRequest, CancellationToken) Create a tenant-scoped cluster variable Create a new cluster variable for the given tenant. CreateUserAsync(UserRequest, CancellationToken) Create user Create a new user. DeleteAuthorizationAsync(AuthorizationKey, CancellationToken) Delete authorization Deletes the authorization with the given key. DeleteDecisionInstanceAsync(DecisionEvaluationKey, DeleteDecisionInstanceRequest, CancellationToken) Delete decision instance Delete all associated decision evaluations based on provided key. DeleteDecisionInstancesBatchOperationAsync(DecisionInstanceDeletionBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken) Delete decision instances (batch) Delete multiple decision instances. This will delete the historic data from secondary storage. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). DeleteDocumentAsync(DocumentId, string?, CancellationToken) Delete document Delete a document from the Camunda 8 cluster. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, Azure, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production) DeleteGlobalClusterVariableAsync(string, CancellationToken) Delete a global-scoped cluster variable Delete a global-scoped cluster variable. DeleteGlobalTaskListenerAsync(GlobalListenerId, CancellationToken) Delete global user task listener Deletes a global user task listener. DeleteGroupAsync(string, CancellationToken) Delete group Deletes the group with the given ID. DeleteMappingRuleAsync(string, CancellationToken) Delete a mapping rule Deletes the mapping rule with the given ID. DeleteProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, DeleteProcessInstanceRequest, CancellationToken) Delete process instance Deletes a process instance. Only instances that are completed or terminated can be deleted. DeleteProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceDeletionBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken) Delete process instances (batch) Delete multiple process instances. This will delete the historic data from secondary storage. Only process instances in a final state (COMPLETED or TERMINATED) can be deleted. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). DeleteResourceAsync(ResourceKey, DeleteResourceRequest, CancellationToken) Delete resource Deletes a deployed resource. This can be a process definition, decision requirements definition, or form definition deployed using the deploy resources endpoint. Specify the resource you want to delete in the resourceKey parameter. Once a resource has been deleted it cannot be recovered. If the resource needs to be available again, a new deployment of the resource is required. By default, only the resource itself is deleted from the runtime state. To also delete the historic data associated with a resource, set the deleteHistory flag in the request body to true. The historic data is deleted asynchronously via a batch operation. The details of the created batch operation are included in the response. Note that history deletion is only supported for process resources; for other resource types this flag is ignored and no history will be deleted. DeleteRoleAsync(string, CancellationToken) Delete role Deletes the role with the given ID. DeleteTenantAsync(TenantId, CancellationToken) Delete tenant Deletes an existing tenant. DeleteTenantClusterVariableAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken) Delete a tenant-scoped cluster variable Delete a tenant-scoped cluster variable. DeleteUserAsync(Username, CancellationToken) Delete user Deletes a user. DeployResourcesFromFilesAsync(string[], string?, CancellationToken) Deploy resources from local filesystem paths. Reads the specified files, infers MIME types from their extensions, and calls CreateDeploymentAsync(MultipartFormDataContent, CancellationToken) with the loaded content. Dispose() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources. DisposeAsync() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources asynchronously. EvaluateConditionalsAsync(ConditionalEvaluationInstruction, CancellationToken) Evaluate root level conditional start events Evaluates root-level conditional start events for process definitions. If the evaluation is successful, it will return the keys of all created process instances, along with their associated process definition key. Multiple root-level conditional start events of the same process definition can trigger if their conditions evaluate to true. You specify the decision to evaluate either by using its unique key (as returned by DeployResource), or using the decision ID. When using the decision ID, the latest deployed version of the decision is used. Supports references to tenant scoped cluster variables when a tenant ID is provided. FailJobAsync(JobKey, JobFailRequest, CancellationToken) Fail job Mark the job as failed. Note that this is currently supported for document stores of type: AWS, Azure, GCP, in-memory (non-production), local (non-production) GetElementInstanceAsync(ElementInstanceKey, ConsistencyOptions<ElementInstanceResult>?, CancellationToken) Get element instance Returns element instance as JSON. GetGlobalClusterVariableAsync(string, ConsistencyOptions<ClusterVariableResult>?, CancellationToken) Get a global-scoped cluster variable Get a global-scoped cluster variable. GetGlobalJobStatisticsAsync(DateTimeOffset, DateTimeOffset, string?, ConsistencyOptions<GlobalJobStatisticsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Global job statistics Returns global aggregated counts for jobs. Optionally filter by the creation time window and/or jobType. GetGlobalTaskListenerAsync(GlobalListenerId, ConsistencyOptions<GlobalTaskListenerResult>?, CancellationToken) Get global user task listener Get a global user task listener by its id. GetIncidentAsync(IncidentKey, ConsistencyOptions<IncidentResult>?, CancellationToken) Get incident Returns incident as JSON. GetJobErrorStatisticsAsync(JobErrorStatisticsQuery, ConsistencyOptions<JobErrorStatisticsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get error metrics for a job type Returns aggregated metrics per error for the given jobType. GetJobTimeSeriesStatisticsAsync(JobTimeSeriesStatisticsQuery, ConsistencyOptions<JobTimeSeriesStatisticsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get time-series metrics for a job type Returns a list of time-bucketed metrics ordered ascending by time. The from and to fields select the time window of interest. Each item in the response corresponds to one time bucket of the requested resolution. GetJobTypeStatisticsAsync(JobTypeStatisticsQuery, ConsistencyOptions<JobTypeStatisticsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get job statistics by type Get statistics about jobs, grouped by job type. GetJobWorkerStatisticsAsync(JobWorkerStatisticsQuery, ConsistencyOptions<JobWorkerStatisticsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get job statistics by worker Get statistics about jobs, grouped by worker, for a given job type. GetLicenseAsync(CancellationToken) Get license status Obtains the status of the current Camunda license. GetMappingRuleAsync(string, ConsistencyOptions<MappingRuleResult>?, CancellationToken) Get a mapping rule Gets the mapping rule with the given ID. GetProcessDefinitionAsync(ProcessDefinitionKey, ConsistencyOptions<ProcessDefinitionResult>?, CancellationToken) Get process definition Returns process definition as JSON. GetProcessDefinitionInstanceStatisticsAsync(ProcessDefinitionInstanceStatisticsQuery, ConsistencyOptions<ProcessDefinitionInstanceStatisticsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get process instance statistics Get statistics about process instances, grouped by process definition and tenant. GetProcessDefinitionInstanceVersionStatisticsAsync(ProcessDefinitionInstanceVersionStatisticsQuery, ConsistencyOptions<ProcessDefinitionInstanceVersionStatisticsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get process instance statistics by version Get statistics about process instances, grouped by version for a given process definition. The process definition ID must be provided as a required field in the request body filter. GetProcessDefinitionMessageSubscriptionStatisticsAsync(ProcessDefinitionMessageSubscriptionStatisticsQuery, ConsistencyOptions<ProcessDefinitionMessageSubscriptionStatisticsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get message subscription statistics Get message subscription statistics, grouped by process definition. GetProcessDefinitionStatisticsAsync(ProcessDefinitionKey, ProcessDefinitionElementStatisticsQuery, ConsistencyOptions<ProcessDefinitionElementStatisticsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get process definition statistics Get statistics about elements in currently running process instances by process definition key and search filter. GetProcessDefinitionXmlAsync(ProcessDefinitionKey, ConsistencyOptions<object>?, CancellationToken) Get process definition XML Returns process definition as XML. GetProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ConsistencyOptions<ProcessInstanceResult>?, CancellationToken) Get process instance Get the process instance by the process instance key. GetProcessInstanceCallHierarchyAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ConsistencyOptions<object>?, CancellationToken) Get call hierarchy Returns the call hierarchy for a given process instance, showing its ancestry up to the root instance. GetProcessInstanceSequenceFlowsAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ConsistencyOptions<ProcessInstanceSequenceFlowsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get sequence flows Get sequence flows taken by the process instance. GetProcessInstanceStatisticsAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ConsistencyOptions<ProcessInstanceElementStatisticsQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get element instance statistics Get statistics about elements by the process instance key. GetProcessInstanceStatisticsByDefinitionAsync(IncidentProcessInstanceStatisticsByDefinitionQuery, ConsistencyOptions<IncidentProcessInstanceStatisticsByDefinitionQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get process instance statistics by definition Returns statistics for active process instances with incidents, grouped by process definition. The result set is scoped to a specific incident error hash code, which must be provided as a filter in the request body. GetProcessInstanceStatisticsByErrorAsync(IncidentProcessInstanceStatisticsByErrorQuery, ConsistencyOptions<IncidentProcessInstanceStatisticsByErrorQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Get process instance statistics by error Returns statistics for active process instances that currently have active incidents, grouped by incident error hash code. GetResourceAsync(ResourceKey, CancellationToken) Get resource Returns a deployed resource. :::info Currently, this endpoint only supports RPA resources. ::: GetResourceContentAsync(ResourceKey, CancellationToken) Get resource content Returns the content of a deployed resource. :::info Currently, this endpoint only supports RPA resources. ::: GetRoleAsync(string, ConsistencyOptions<RoleResult>?, CancellationToken) Get role Get a role by its ID. GetStartProcessFormAsync(ProcessDefinitionKey, ConsistencyOptions<FormResult>?, CancellationToken) Get process start form Get the start form of a process. Note that this endpoint will only return linked forms. This endpoint does not support embedded forms. GetStatusAsync(CancellationToken) Get cluster status Checks the health status of the cluster by verifying if there's at least one partition with a healthy leader. GetSystemConfigurationAsync(CancellationToken) System configuration (alpha) Returns the current system configuration. The response is an envelope that groups settings by feature area. This endpoint is an alpha feature and may be subject to change in future releases. GetTenantAsync(TenantId, ConsistencyOptions<TenantResult>?, CancellationToken) Get tenant Retrieves a single tenant by tenant ID. GetTenantClusterVariableAsync(TenantId, string, ConsistencyOptions<ClusterVariableResult>?, CancellationToken) Get a tenant-scoped cluster variable Get a tenant-scoped cluster variable. GetTopologyAsync(CancellationToken) Get cluster topology Obtains the current topology of the cluster the gateway is part of. GetUsageMetricsAsync(DateTimeOffset, DateTimeOffset, TenantId?, bool?, ConsistencyOptions<UsageMetricsResponse>?, CancellationToken) Get usage metrics Retrieve the usage metrics based on given criteria. GetUserAsync(Username, ConsistencyOptions<GetUserResponse>?, CancellationToken) Get user Get a user by its username. GetUserTaskAsync(UserTaskKey, ConsistencyOptions<UserTaskResult>?, CancellationToken) Get user task Get the user task by the user task key. GetUserTaskFormAsync(UserTaskKey, ConsistencyOptions<FormResult>?, CancellationToken) Get user task form Get the form of a user task. Note that this endpoint will only return linked forms. This endpoint does not support embedded forms. GetVariableAsync(VariableKey, ConsistencyOptions<VariableResult>?, CancellationToken) Get variable Get a variable by its key. This endpoint returns both process-level and local (element-scoped) variables. The variable's scopeKey indicates whether it's a process-level variable or scoped to a specific element instance. GetWorkers() Returns a snapshot of all registered workers. MigrateProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ProcessInstanceMigrationInstruction, CancellationToken) Migrate process instance Migrates a process instance to a new process definition. This request can contain multiple mapping instructions to define mapping between the active process instance's elements and target process definition elements. Use this to upgrade a process instance to a new version of a process or to a different process definition, e.g. to keep your running instances up-to-date with the latest process improvements. MigrateProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceMigrationBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken) Migrate process instances (batch) Migrate multiple process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can be migrated, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). ModifyProcessInstanceAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, ProcessInstanceModificationInstruction, CancellationToken) Modify process instance Modifies a running process instance. This request can contain multiple instructions to activate an element of the process or to terminate an active instance of an element. Use this to repair a process instance that is stuck on an element or took an unintended path. For example, because an external system is not available or doesn't respond as expected. ModifyProcessInstancesBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceModificationBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken) Modify process instances (batch) Modify multiple process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can be modified, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. In contrast to single modification operation, it is not possible to add variable instructions or modify by element key. It is only possible to use the element id of the source and target. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). PinClockAsync(ClockPinRequest, CancellationToken) Pin internal clock (alpha) Set a precise, static time for the Zeebe engine's internal clock. When the clock is pinned, it remains at the specified time and does not advance. To change the time, the clock must be pinned again with a new timestamp. This endpoint is an alpha feature and may be subject to change in future releases. PublishMessageAsync(MessagePublicationRequest, CancellationToken) Publish message Publishes a single message. Messages are published to specific partitions computed from their correlation keys. Messages can be buffered. The endpoint does not wait for a correlation result. Use the message correlation endpoint for such use cases. ResetClockAsync(CancellationToken) Reset internal clock (alpha) Resets the Zeebe engine's internal clock to the current system time, enabling it to tick in real-time. This operation is useful for returning the clock to normal behavior after it has been pinned to a specific time. This endpoint is an alpha feature and may be subject to change in future releases. ResolveIncidentAsync(IncidentKey, IncidentResolutionRequest, CancellationToken) Resolve incident Marks the incident as resolved; most likely a call to Update job will be necessary to reset the job's retries, followed by this call. ResolveIncidentsBatchOperationAsync(ProcessInstanceIncidentResolutionBatchOperationRequest, CancellationToken) Resolve related incidents (batch) Resolves multiple instances of process instances. Since only process instances with ACTIVE state can have unresolved incidents, any given filters for state are ignored and overridden during this batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batchOperationKey from the response and the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). ResolveProcessInstanceIncidentsAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, CancellationToken) Resolve related incidents Creates a batch operation to resolve multiple incidents of a process instance. ResumeBatchOperationAsync(BatchOperationKey, CancellationToken) Resume Batch operation Resumes a suspended batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). RunWorkersAsync(TimeSpan?, CancellationToken) Block until cancellation is requested, keeping all registered workers alive. This is the typical entry point for worker-only applications. When the token is cancelled, all workers are stopped gracefully. SearchAuditLogsAsync(AuditLogSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<AuditLogSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search audit logs Search for audit logs based on given criteria. SearchAuthorizationsAsync(AuthorizationSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<AuthorizationSearchResult>?, CancellationToken) Search authorizations Search for authorizations based on given criteria. SearchBatchOperationItemsAsync(BatchOperationItemSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<BatchOperationItemSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search batch operation items Search for batch operation items based on given criteria. SearchBatchOperationsAsync(BatchOperationSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<BatchOperationSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search batch operations Search for batch operations based on given criteria. SearchClientsForGroupAsync(string, SearchClientsForGroupRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchClientsForGroupResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search group clients Search clients assigned to a group. SearchClientsForRoleAsync(string, SearchClientsForRoleRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchClientsForRoleResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search role clients Search clients with assigned role. SearchClientsForTenantAsync(TenantId, SearchClientsForTenantRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchClientsForTenantResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search clients for tenant Retrieves a filtered and sorted list of clients for a specified tenant. SearchClusterVariablesAsync(ClusterVariableSearchQueryRequest, bool?, ConsistencyOptions<ClusterVariableSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search for cluster variables based on given criteria. By default, long variable values in the response are truncated. SearchCorrelatedMessageSubscriptionsAsync(CorrelatedMessageSubscriptionSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<CorrelatedMessageSubscriptionSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search correlated message subscriptions Search correlated message subscriptions based on given criteria. SearchDecisionDefinitionsAsync(DecisionDefinitionSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<DecisionDefinitionSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search decision definitions Search for decision definitions based on given criteria. SearchDecisionInstancesAsync(DecisionInstanceSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<DecisionInstanceSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search decision instances Search for decision instances based on given criteria. SearchDecisionRequirementsAsync(DecisionRequirementsSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<DecisionRequirementsSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search decision requirements Search for decision requirements based on given criteria. SearchElementInstanceIncidentsAsync(ElementInstanceKey, IncidentSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<IncidentSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search for incidents of a specific element instance Search for incidents caused by the specified element instance, including incidents of any child instances created from this element instance. Although the elementInstanceKey is provided as a path parameter to indicate the root element instance, you may also include an elementInstanceKey within the filter object to narrow results to specific child element instances. This is useful, for example, if you want to isolate incidents associated with nested or subordinate elements within the given element instance while excluding incidents directly tied to the root element itself. SearchElementInstancesAsync(ElementInstanceSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<ElementInstanceSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search element instances Search for element instances based on given criteria. SearchGlobalTaskListenersAsync(GlobalTaskListenerSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<GlobalTaskListenerSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search global user task listeners Search for global user task listeners based on given criteria. SearchGroupIdsForTenantAsync(TenantId, TenantGroupSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<TenantGroupSearchResult>?, CancellationToken) Search groups for tenant Retrieves a filtered and sorted list of groups for a specified tenant. SearchGroupsAsync(GroupSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<GroupSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search groups Search for groups based on given criteria. SearchGroupsForRoleAsync(string, RoleGroupSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<RoleGroupSearchResult>?, CancellationToken) Search role groups Search groups with assigned role. SearchIncidentsAsync(IncidentSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<IncidentSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search incidents Search for incidents based on given criteria. SearchJobsAsync(JobSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<JobSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search jobs Search for jobs based on given criteria. SearchMappingRuleAsync(MappingRuleSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchMappingRuleResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search mapping rules Search for mapping rules based on given criteria. SearchMappingRulesForGroupAsync(string, MappingRuleSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchMappingRulesForGroupResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search group mapping rules Search mapping rules assigned to a group. SearchMappingRulesForRoleAsync(string, MappingRuleSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchMappingRulesForRoleResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search role mapping rules Search mapping rules with assigned role. SearchMappingRulesForTenantAsync(TenantId, MappingRuleSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchMappingRulesForTenantResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search mapping rules for tenant Retrieves a filtered and sorted list of MappingRules for a specified tenant. SearchMessageSubscriptionsAsync(MessageSubscriptionSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<MessageSubscriptionSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search message subscriptions Search for message subscriptions based on given criteria. SearchProcessDefinitionsAsync(ProcessDefinitionSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<ProcessDefinitionSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search process definitions Search for process definitions based on given criteria. SearchProcessInstanceIncidentsAsync(ProcessInstanceKey, IncidentSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<IncidentSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search related incidents Search for incidents caused by the process instance or any of its called process or decision instances. Although the processInstanceKey is provided as a path parameter to indicate the root process instance, you may also include a processInstanceKey within the filter object to narrow results to specific child process instances. This is useful, for example, if you want to isolate incidents associated with subprocesses or called processes under the root instance while excluding incidents directly tied to the root. SearchProcessInstancesAsync(ProcessInstanceSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<ProcessInstanceSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search process instances Search for process instances based on given criteria. SearchRolesAsync(RoleSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<RoleSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search roles Search for roles based on given criteria. SearchRolesForGroupAsync(string, RoleSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchRolesForGroupResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search group roles Search roles assigned to a group. SearchRolesForTenantAsync(TenantId, RoleSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchRolesForTenantResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search roles for tenant Retrieves a filtered and sorted list of roles for a specified tenant. SearchTenantsAsync(TenantSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<TenantSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search tenants Retrieves a filtered and sorted list of tenants. SearchUserTaskAuditLogsAsync(UserTaskKey, UserTaskAuditLogSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<AuditLogSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search user task audit logs Search for user task audit logs based on given criteria. SearchUserTaskEffectiveVariablesAsync(UserTaskKey, SearchUserTaskEffectiveVariablesRequest, bool?, ConsistencyOptions<VariableSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search user task effective variables Search for the effective variables of a user task. This endpoint returns deduplicated variables where each variable name appears at most once. When the same variable name exists at multiple scope levels in the scope hierarchy, the value from the innermost scope (closest to the user task) takes precedence. This is useful for retrieving the actual runtime state of variables as seen by the user task. By default, long variable values in the response are truncated. SearchUserTaskVariablesAsync(UserTaskKey, SearchUserTaskVariablesRequest, bool?, ConsistencyOptions<VariableSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search user task variables Search for user task variables based on given criteria. This endpoint returns all variable documents visible from the user task's scope, including variables from parent scopes in the scope hierarchy. If the same variable name exists at multiple scope levels, each scope's variable is returned as a separate result. Use the /user-tasks/{userTaskKey}/effective-variables/search endpoint to get deduplicated variables where the innermost scope takes precedence. By default, long variable values in the response are truncated. SearchUserTasksAsync(UserTaskSearchQuery, ConsistencyOptions<UserTaskSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search user tasks Search for user tasks based on given criteria. SearchUsersAsync(UserSearchQueryRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchUsersResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search users Search for users based on given criteria. SearchUsersForGroupAsync(string, SearchUsersForGroupRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchUsersForGroupResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search group users Search users assigned to a group. SearchUsersForRoleAsync(string, SearchUsersForRoleRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchUsersForRoleResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search role users Search users with assigned role. SearchUsersForTenantAsync(TenantId, SearchUsersForTenantRequest, ConsistencyOptions<SearchUsersForTenantResponse>?, CancellationToken) Search users for tenant Retrieves a filtered and sorted list of users for a specified tenant. SearchVariablesAsync(SearchVariablesRequest, bool?, ConsistencyOptions<VariableSearchQueryResult>?, CancellationToken) Search variables Search for variables based on given criteria. This endpoint returns variables that exist directly at the specified scopes - it does not include variables from parent scopes that would be visible through the scope hierarchy. Variables can be process-level (scoped to the process instance) or local (scoped to specific BPMN elements like tasks, subprocesses, etc.). By default, long variable values in the response are truncated. StopAllWorkersAsync(TimeSpan?) Stop all registered workers and wait for in-flight jobs to drain. SuspendBatchOperationAsync(BatchOperationKey, CancellationToken) Suspend Batch operation Suspends a running batch operation. This is done asynchronously, the progress can be tracked using the batch operation status endpoint (/batch-operations/{batchOperationKey}). ThrowJobErrorAsync(JobKey, JobErrorRequest, CancellationToken) Throw error for job Reports a business error (i.e. non-technical) that occurs while processing a job. UnassignClientFromGroupAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Unassign a client from a group Unassigns a client from a group. The client is removed as a group member, with associated authorizations, roles, and tenant assignments no longer applied. UnassignClientFromTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken) Unassign a client from a tenant Unassigns the client from the specified tenant. The client can no longer access tenant data. UnassignGroupFromTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken) Unassign a group from a tenant Unassigns a group from a specified tenant. Members of the group (users, clients) will no longer have access to the tenant's data - except they are assigned directly to the tenant. UnassignMappingRuleFromGroupAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Unassign a mapping rule from a group Unassigns a mapping rule from a group. UnassignMappingRuleFromTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken) Unassign a mapping rule from a tenant Unassigns a single mapping rule from a specified tenant without deleting the rule. UnassignRoleFromClientAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Unassign a role from a client Unassigns the specified role from the client. The client will no longer inherit the authorizations associated with this role. UnassignRoleFromGroupAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Unassign a role from a group Unassigns the specified role from the group. All group members (user or client) no longer inherit the authorizations associated with this role. UnassignRoleFromMappingRuleAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Unassign a role from a mapping rule Unassigns a role from a mapping rule. UnassignRoleFromTenantAsync(TenantId, string, CancellationToken) Unassign a role from a tenant Unassigns a role from a specified tenant. Users, Clients or Groups, that have the role assigned, will no longer have access to the tenant's data - unless they are assigned directly to the tenant. UnassignRoleFromUserAsync(string, Username, CancellationToken) Unassign a role from a user Unassigns a role from a user. The user will no longer inherit the authorizations associated with this role. UnassignUserFromGroupAsync(string, Username, CancellationToken) Unassign a user from a group Unassigns a user from a group. The user is removed as a group member, with associated authorizations, roles, and tenant assignments no longer applied. UnassignUserFromTenantAsync(TenantId, Username, CancellationToken) Unassign a user from a tenant Unassigns the user from the specified tenant. The user can no longer access tenant data. UnassignUserTaskAsync(UserTaskKey, CancellationToken) Unassign user task Removes the assignee of a task with the given key. Unassignment waits for blocking task listeners on this lifecycle transition. If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats. UpdateAuthorizationAsync(AuthorizationKey, AuthorizationRequest, CancellationToken) Update authorization Update the authorization with the given key. UpdateGlobalClusterVariableAsync(string, UpdateClusterVariableRequest, CancellationToken) Update a global-scoped cluster variable Updates the value of an existing global cluster variable. The variable must exist, otherwise a 404 error is returned. UpdateGlobalTaskListenerAsync(GlobalListenerId, UpdateGlobalTaskListenerRequest, CancellationToken) Update global user task listener Updates a global user task listener. UpdateGroupAsync(string, GroupUpdateRequest, CancellationToken) Update group Update a group with the given ID. UpdateJobAsync(JobKey, JobUpdateRequest, CancellationToken) Update job Update a job with the given key. UpdateMappingRuleAsync(string, MappingRuleUpdateRequest, CancellationToken) Update mapping rule Update a mapping rule. UpdateRoleAsync(string, RoleUpdateRequest, CancellationToken) Update role Update a role with the given ID. UpdateTenantAsync(TenantId, TenantUpdateRequest, CancellationToken) Update tenant Updates an existing tenant. UpdateTenantClusterVariableAsync(TenantId, string, UpdateClusterVariableRequest, CancellationToken) Update a tenant-scoped cluster variable Updates the value of an existing tenant-scoped cluster variable. The variable must exist, otherwise a 404 error is returned. UpdateUserAsync(Username, UserUpdateRequest, CancellationToken) Update user Updates a user. UpdateUserTaskAsync(UserTaskKey, UserTaskUpdateRequest, CancellationToken) Update user task Update a user task with the given key. Updates wait for blocking task listeners on this lifecycle transition. If listener processing is delayed beyond the request timeout, this endpoint can return 504. Other gateway timeout causes are also possible. Retry with backoff and inspect listener worker availability and logs when this repeats.

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.Changeset..title: Class Changeset | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.Changeset..summary: Class Changeset Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll JSON object with changed task attribute values. The following attributes can be adjusted with this endpoint, additional attributes will be ignored: candidateGroups - reset by providing an empty list candidateUsers - reset by providing an empty list dueDate - reset by providing an empty String followUpDate - reset by providing an empty String priority - minimum 0, maximum 100, default 50 Providing any of those attributes with a null value or omitting it preserves the persisted attribute's value. The assignee cannot be adjusted with this endpoint, use the Assign task endpoint. This ensures correct event emission for assignee changes. public sealed class Changeset Inheritance object Changeset Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods TypedVariables.DeserializeAs<T>(object?, JsonSerializerOptions?) Properties CandidateGroups The list of candidate groups of the task. Reset by providing an empty list. CandidateUsers The list of candidate users of the task. Reset by providing an empty list. DueDate The due date of the task. Reset by providing an empty String. FollowUpDate The follow-up date of the task. Reset by providing an empty String. Priority The priority of the task.

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.JobResultU.summary: Property Denied Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll Denied Indicates whether the worker denies the work, i.e. explicitly doesn't approve it. For example, a user task listener can deny the completion of a task by setting this flag to true. In this example, the completion of a task is represented by a job that the worker can complete as denied. As a result, the completion request is rejected and the task remains active. Defaults to false. [JsonPropertyName("denied")] public bool? Denied { get; set; } Property Value bool?

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.JobWorker..title: Class JobWorker | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.JobWorker..summary: Class JobWorker Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll A long-running worker that polls the Camunda broker for jobs of a specific type, dispatches them to a handler, and auto-completes or auto-fails based on the outcome. Concurrency model: jobs are dispatched as concurrent Tasks on the .NET thread pool. MaxConcurrentJobs controls how many jobs may be in-flight simultaneously. For async handlers (the typical case), the thread pool thread is released during await points, so many jobs can be handled by a small number of OS threads. For CPU-bound handlers, set MaxConcurrentJobs to ProcessorCount to match available cores. public sealed class JobWorker : IAsyncDisposable, IDisposable Inheritance object JobWorker Implements IAsyncDisposable IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods TypedVariables.DeserializeAs<T>(object?, JsonSerializerOptions?) Properties ActiveJobs Number of jobs currently being processed. IsRunning Whether the poll loop is currently running. Name The worker's name (auto-generated or from config). Methods Dispose() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources. DisposeAsync() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources asynchronously. Start() Start the polling loop. No-op if already running. StopAsync(TimeSpan?) Stop the polling loop and optionally wait for in-flight jobs to drain.

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.JobWorkerC.title: Property MaxConcurrentJobs | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.JobWorkerC.summary: Property MaxConcurrentJobs Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll MaxConcurrentJobs Maximum number of jobs that may be in-flight (activated and being handled) concurrently by this worker. Controls how many jobs are requested per poll and how many handler tasks run in parallel. For I/O-bound handlers (HTTP calls, database queries), higher values (32–128) improve throughput because async handlers release threads during awaits. For CPU-bound handlers, set to ProcessorCount or lower to avoid over-subscribing the thread pool. Set to 1 for sequential (single-job-at-a-time) processing. public int MaxConcurrentJobs { get; init; } Property Value int

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.JobWorkerC.summary: Class JobWorkerConfig Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll Configuration for a JobWorker. public sealed class JobWorkerConfig Inheritance object JobWorkerConfig Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods TypedVariables.DeserializeAs<T>(object?, JsonSerializerOptions?) Properties AutoStart Whether to start polling immediately on creation. Default: true. FetchVariables Variable names to fetch from the process instance scope. null = fetch all. JobTimeoutMs How long (in ms) the job is reserved for this worker before the broker makes it available to other workers. JobType The BPMN job type to subscribe to (e.g. "payment-service"). MaxConcurrentJobs Maximum number of jobs that may be in-flight (activated and being handled) concurrently by this worker. Controls how many jobs are requested per poll and how many handler tasks run in parallel. For I/O-bound handlers (HTTP calls, database queries), higher values (32–128) improve throughput because async handlers release threads during awaits. For CPU-bound handlers, set to ProcessorCount or lower to avoid over-subscribing the thread pool. Set to 1 for sequential (single-job-at-a-time) processing. PollIntervalMs Delay (in ms) between poll cycles when no jobs are available or when at capacity. Default: 500 ms. PollTimeoutMs Long-poll timeout (in ms) sent to the broker. The broker holds the activation request open until jobs are available or this timeout elapses. null or 0 = broker default; negative = long polling disabled. StartupJitterMaxSeconds Maximum random delay (in seconds) before the worker starts polling. When multiple application instances restart simultaneously, this spreads out initial activation requests to avoid saturating the server. 0 (the default) means no delay. WorkerName Worker name sent to the broker for logging and diagnostics. Auto-generated if not set.

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.ProcessDef.title: Property Or | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.ProcessDef.summary:
Property Or Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll Or Defines a list of alternative filter groups combined using OR logic. Each object in the array is evaluated independently, and the filter matches if any one of them is satisfied. Top-level fields and the $or clause are combined using AND logic — meaning: (top-level filters) AND (any of the $or filters) must match.
Example: { "state": "ACTIVE", "tenantId": 123, "$or": [ { "processDefinitionId": "process_v1" }, { "processDefinitionId": "process_v2", "hasIncident": true } ] } This matches process instances that:
  • are in ACTIVE state
  • have tenant id equal to 123
  • and match either:
    • processDefinitionId is process_v1, or
    • processDefinitionId is process_v2 and hasIncident is true

Note: Using complex $or conditions may impact performance, use with caution in high-volume environments. [JsonPropertyName("$or")] public List? Or { get; set; } Property Value List

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.ProcessIns.title: Property Or | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.ProcessIns.summary:
Property Or Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll Or Defines a list of alternative filter groups combined using OR logic. Each object in the array is evaluated independently, and the filter matches if any one of them is satisfied. Top-level fields and the $or clause are combined using AND logic — meaning: (top-level filters) AND (any of the $or filters) must match.
Example: { "state": "ACTIVE", "tenantId": 123, "$or": [ { "processDefinitionId": "process_v1" }, { "processDefinitionId": "process_v2", "hasIncident": true } ] } This matches process instances that:
  • are in ACTIVE state
  • have tenant id equal to 123
  • and match either:
    • processDefinitionId is process_v1, or
    • processDefinitionId is process_v2 and hasIncident is true

Note: Using complex $or conditions may impact performance, use with caution in high-volume environments. [JsonPropertyName("$or")] public List? Or { get; set; } Property Value List

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.ProcessIns.title: Class ProcessInstanceFilter | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.ProcessIns.summary:
Class ProcessInstanceFilter Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll Process instance search filter. public sealed class ProcessInstanceFilter Inheritance object ProcessInstanceFilter Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods TypedVariables.DeserializeAs(object?, JsonSerializerOptions?) Properties Or Defines a list of alternative filter groups combined using OR logic. Each object in the array is evaluated independently, and the filter matches if any one of them is satisfied. Top-level fields and the $or clause are combined using AND logic — meaning: (top-level filters) AND (any of the $or filters) must match.
Example: { "state": "ACTIVE", "tenantId": 123, "$or": [ { "processDefinitionId": "process_v1" }, { "processDefinitionId": "process_v2", "hasIncident": true } ] } This matches process instances that:
  • are in ACTIVE state
  • have tenant id equal to 123
  • and match either:
    • processDefinitionId is process_v1, or
    • processDefinitionId is process_v2 and hasIncident is true

Note: Using complex $or conditions may impact performance, use with caution in high-volume environments. ProcessDefinitionId The process definition id. ProcessDefinitionKey The process definition key. ProcessDefinitionName The process definition name. ProcessDefinitionVersion The process definition version. ProcessDefinitionVersionTag The process definition version tag.

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.SetVariabl.title: Property Local | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.SetVariabl.summary: Property Local Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll Local If set to true, the variables are merged strictly into the local scope (as specified by the elementInstanceKey). Otherwise, the variables are propagated to upper scopes and set at the outermost one. Let's consider the following example: There are two scopes '1' and '2'. Scope '1' is the parent scope of '2'. The effective variables of the scopes are: 1 => { "foo" : 2 } 2 => { "bar" : 1 } An update request with elementInstanceKey as '2', variables { "foo": 5 }, and local set to true leaves scope '1' unchanged and adjusts scope '2' to { "bar": 1, "foo": 5 }. By default, with local set to false, scope '1' will be { "foo": 5 } and scope '2' will be { "bar": 1 }. [JsonPropertyName("local")] public bool? Local { get; set; } Property Value bool?

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.TypedVaria.title: Method DeserializeAs | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.TypedVaria.summary: Method DeserializeAs Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll DeserializeAs<T>(object?, JsonSerializerOptions?) Deserializes a Camunda variable or custom header payload to the specified type. Works on object properties that are JsonElement at runtime (the standard shape returned by the Camunda REST API). public static T? DeserializeAs<T>(this object? payload, JsonSerializerOptions? options = null) Parameters payload object The Variables or CustomHeaders property value from a Camunda API response. options JsonSerializerOptions Optional JSON serializer options. When null, uses default options with camelCase naming to match Camunda's JSON format. Returns T The deserialized DTO, or default if the payload is null. Type Parameters T The target DTO type to deserialize into.

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.TypedVaria.title: Class TypedVariables | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.TypedVaria.summary: Class TypedVariables Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll Extension methods for deserializing Camunda variable and custom header payloads from untyped object properties into strongly-typed DTOs. Camunda API responses return variables and customHeaders as object properties which, at runtime, are JsonElement values. These extensions let you opt in to typed deserialization: // Define your domain DTO public record OrderVars(string OrderId, decimal Amount); // Deserialize variables from a process instance result var result = await client.CreateProcessInstanceAsync( new ProcessInstanceCreationInstructionById { ProcessDefinitionId = ProcessDefinitionId.AssumeExists("order-process"), Variables = new OrderVars("ord-123", 99.99m), // input: just assign your DTO }); var vars = result.Variables.DeserializeAs<OrderVars>(); // output: typed extraction For input (sending variables), simply assign your DTO to the Variables property — System.Text.Json serializes the runtime type automatically. For output (receiving variables), call DeserializeAs<T>(object?, JsonSerializerOptions?) on the Variables or CustomHeaders property to deserialize the underlying JsonElement into your DTO type. public static class TypedVariables Inheritance object TypedVariables Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods DeserializeAs<T>(object?, JsonSerializerOptions?) Deserializes a Camunda variable or custom header payload to the specified type. Works on object properties that are JsonElement at runtime (the standard shape returned by the Camunda REST API).

api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.UserTaskUp.title: Property Changeset | Camunda C# SDK
api/Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.UserTaskUp.summary: Property Changeset Namespace Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Assembly Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.dll Changeset JSON object with changed task attribute values. The following attributes can be adjusted with this endpoint, additional attributes will be ignored: candidateGroups - reset by providing an empty list candidateUsers - reset by providing an empty list dueDate - reset by providing an empty String followUpDate - reset by providing an empty String priority - minimum 0, maximum 100, default 50 Providing any of those attributes with a null value or omitting it preserves the persisted attribute's value. The assignee cannot be adjusted with this endpoint, use the Assign task endpoint. This ensures correct event emission for assignee changes. [JsonPropertyName("changeset")] public Changeset? Changeset { get; set; } Property Value Changeset

TypedVariables Extension methods for deserializing Camunda variable and custom header payloads from untyped object properties into strongly-typed DTOs. Camunda API responses return variables and customHeaders as object properties which, at runtime, are JsonElement values. These extensions let you opt in to typed deserialization: // Define your domain DTO public record OrderVars(string OrderId, decimal Amount); // Deserialize variables from a process instance result var result = await client.CreateProcessInstanceAsync( new ProcessInstanceCreationInstructionById { ProcessDefinitionId = ProcessDefinitionId.AssumeExists("order-process"), Variables = new OrderVars("ord-123", 99.99m), // input: just assign your DTO }); var vars = result.Variables.DeserializeAs<OrderVars>(); // output: typed extraction For input (sending variables), simply assign your DTO to the Variables property — System.Text.Json serializes the runtime type automatically. For output (receiving variables), call DeserializeAs<T>(object?, JsonSerializerOptions?) on the Variables or CustomHeaders property to deserialize the underlying JsonElement into your DTO type. Delegates JobHandler Delegate for job handler functions. Return the output variables to complete the job with, or null to complete with no output variables. To signal a BPMN error, throw BpmnErrorException. To explicitly fail a job with custom retries, throw JobFailureException. Any other unhandled exception auto-fails the job with retries - 1.

articles/getting-started.html.title: Getting Started | Camunda C# SDK
articles/getting-started.html.summary: Getting Started Installation dotnet add package Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk Basic Usage using Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk; // Reads CAMUNDA_* environment variables automatically using var client = CamundaClient.Create(); Configuration Set environment variables to configure the client: export CAMUNDA_REST_ADDRESS=https://your-cluster.camunda.io export CAMUNDA_CLIENT_ID=your-client-id export CAMUNDA_CLIENT_SECRET=your-secret export CAMUNDA_OAUTH_URL=https://login.cloud.camunda.io/oauth/token export CAMUNDA_TOKEN_AUDIENCE=zeebe.camunda.io Or configure programmatically: using Camunda.Orchestration.Sdk.Runtime; using var client = CamundaClient.Create(new CamundaOptions { Config = new Dictionary<string, string> { ["CAMUNDA_REST_ADDRESS"] = "https://your-cluster.camunda.io", ["CAMUNDA_CLIENT_ID"] = "your-client-id", ["CAMUNDA_CLIENT_SECRET"] = "your-secret", ["CAMUNDA_OAUTH_URL"] = "https://login.cloud.camunda.io/oauth/token", ["CAMUNDA_TOKEN_AUDIENCE"] = "zeebe.camunda.io", }, }); Authentication The SDK supports three auth strategies: OAuth — Automatic token management with singleflight refresh Basic — HTTP Basic Authentication None — No authentication (local development) Auth strategy is auto-detected when CAMUNDA_CLIENT_ID, CAMUNDA_CLIENT_SECRET, and CAMUNDA_OAUTH_URL are all set. Error Handling All API errors throw typed exceptions: try { // var result = await client.GetProcessInstanceAsync(key); } catch (HttpSdkException ex) when (ex.Status == 404) { Console.WriteLine($"Not found: {ex.Detail}"); } catch (EventualConsistencyTimeoutException ex) { Console.WriteLine($"Timed out after {ex.WaitedMs}ms"); }

index.html.summary: Camunda C# SDK — API Documentation Welcome to the API reference for the Camunda 8 Orchestration Cluster API C# SDK. Quick Links API Reference Getting Started GitHub Repository NuGet Package Overview This SDK provides a typed C# client for the Camunda 8 REST API, auto-generated from the upstream OpenAPI specification with ergonomic wrappers including: Unified configuration from environment variables (CAMUNDA_*) OAuth and Basic authentication with automatic token management HTTP retry with exponential backoff and jitter Backpressure management with adaptive concurrency Eventual consistency polling for search endpoints
